Anhui Hengyuan Coal Industry and Electricity Power Co.,Ltd Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Anhui Hengyuan Coal Industry and Electricity Power Co.,Ltd reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 3,781.53 million compared to CNY 5,407.96 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 3,781.53 million compared to CNY 5,407.96 million a year ago.

Net loss was CNY 99.72 million compared to net income of CNY 944.77 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.0831 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of CNY 0.7873 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.0831 compared to diluted earnings per share from continuing operations of CNY 0.7873 a year ago.

Transunion Raises Earnings Guidance for the Twelve Months Ending December 31, 2025
data.description: TransUnion raised earnings guidance for the twelve months ending December 31, 2025. For the twelve months ending December 31, 2025, the company now expects to deliver 8% to 8.5% revenue growth and expects revenue, as reported to be in range of $4,524 million to $4,544 million, Net income attributable to TransUnion to be range of $438 million to $448 million and Diluted Earnings per Share to be in range of $2.21 to $2.26.

Prada up 9% over nine months, with signs of an improvement in China
data.description:
On Thursday Italian luxury group Prada announced a 9% increase in net sales for the first nine months of the year, driven by momentum in the US and Asia-Pacific markets. The company also reported "some improvement" in the situation in China during the third quarter.

The main driver of this growth remains Miu Miu, the group's youngest brand, which posted a strong 29% rise in retail sales over the quarter. Conversely, the main Prada brand's sales slipped 1% over the same period, although this represented a sequential improvement.

"The group has just completed its 19th consecutive quarter of growth," said Patrizio Bertelli, Prada's chairman, praising the company's resilience in a global environment that remains uncertain.

Retail sales account for 90% of the group's total revenue, which reached €4.07bn for the period of January to September.

TechnipFMC plc Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
TechnipFMC plc reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was USD 2,647.3 million compared to USD 2,348.4 million a year ago. Net income was USD 309.7 million compared to USD 274.6 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.76 compared to USD 0.64 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.75 compared to USD 0.63 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was USD 7,415.6 million compared to USD 6,716 million a year ago. Net income was USD 721.2 million compared to USD 618.2 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.74 compared to USD 1.44 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.71 compared to USD 1.4 a year ago.

Hasbro raises annual targets after Q3 better than expected
data.description:
Hasbro announced on Thursday that it had raised its financial targets for 2025 after posting better-than-expected Q3 results, mainly due to the record performance of its card game Magic: The Gathering.

The American toy and board game manufacturer's revenue increased by 8% to nearly $1.39bn in the past quarter, driven by a 55% jump in sales of Magic, which benefited from the release of the "At the Gates of Eternity" and "Spider-Man" editions.

Despite the impact of customs surcharges, operating income rose 13% to $341m, thanks to cost-cutting and productivity improvement measures taken by management, resulting in adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.68.

By comparison, the market consensus was for EPS of $1.66 on quarterly sales of $1.35bn.

Following these better-than-expected results, the Pawtucket, Rhode Island-based group now says it is targeting revenue growth of 5% to 10% at constant exchange rates this year, compared with a previous forecast of "around 5%."

Its adjusted operating income (EBITDA) is expected to be between $1.24bn and $1.26bn, compared with $1.17bn to $1.20bn previously.

Orion Signs Exclusive Licensing Agreement with Abzena for Cancer Antibody
data.description:
Finnish pharmaceutical company Orion has secured an exclusive commercial license for a monoclonal antibody developed by Abzena, targeting a cancer with significant unmet medical need.

The antibody, created at Abzena's research facility in Cambridge using the company's proprietary CHAb technology, is set to strengthen Orion's oncology research portfolio. Abzena utilized its AbZelectPRO platform to develop a stable production cell line for the antibody.

data.title: Earnings Flash (ESQ) Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. Posts Q3 Adjusted EPS $1.47 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $1.47
SAP: stock falls with cloud forecasts disappointing
data.description:
SAP fell more than 1% in Frankfurt, following a downward revision by the software publisher of its cloud revenue forecasts when it published its results for Q3  2025.

The German group now expects cloud revenues to be at the lower end of its target range of €21.6bn to €21.9bn at constant exchange rates for the full year.

This revision overshadows more optimistic expectations for its non-IFRS operating profit, for which it is now targeting the upper end of €10.3bn-$10.6bn, and its free cash flow, which it now expects to be between €8bn and €8.2bn (rather than around €8bn).

In the first nine months of 2025, SAP's non-IFRS EPS rose 44% to €4.53, while its operating profit increased 35% to €7.59bn, with revenues up 11% to €27.1bn (+27% to €15.4bn for the cloud).

data.title: Honeywell raises annual EPS target range
data.description:
In its quarterly report, Honeywell announced that it was updating its targets for 2025, now forecasting adjusted EPS of between $10.60 and $10.70, instead of the previous target range of $10.45 to $10.65.

However, the industrial conglomerate has lowered its annual margin expectations for its segments to 22.9%-23% (down from 23%-23.2%) and its revenue expectations to $40.7bn-$40.9bn (down from $40.8bn-$41.3bn previously).

In Q3, it achieved adjusted EPS up 9% to $2.82, beating the consensus, with segment margin down 0.5 points to 23.1% on revenue growth of 7% to $10.41bn (+6% in organic terms).

"Increased orders in our businesses have pushed the group's order book to a new record level," said CEO Vimal Kapur, as he moves forward with his plan to split the company into three independent entities.
